eligibility: Age of joining and contribution period The minimum age of joining APY is 18 years and maximum is 40 years. The age of exit and start of pension is 60 years. Subscriber contribution to APY shall be made through the facility of 'auto-debit' of the prescribed contribution amount from the savings bank account of the subscriber on monthly, quarterly or half-yearly basis. Income tax payers are not eligible for APY (effective October 2022). The subscribers are required to contribute the prescribed contribution amount from the age of joining APY till the age of 60 years.
tags: Pension, Unorganized Workers, Bank Account Holders

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
eligibility: Rural Beneficiaries Out of the total seven deprivation criteria for rural areas, PM-JAY covered all such families who fall into at least one of the following six deprivation criteria and automatic inclusion: Only one room with kucha walls and kucha roof No adult member between ages 16 to 59 Households with no adult male member between ages 16 to 59 Disabled member and no able bodied adult member SC/ST households Landless households deriving major part of their income from manual casual labour. Urban poor families meeting occupational criteria are also covered. Aadhaar is required for golden card issuance.

The applicant should be a resident of Bihar or should have been residing in Bihar for at least the past 10 years as of the date of application submission. The applicant should be physically disabled. The applicant should possess a disability certificate with a minimum of 40% disability. There is no minimum or maximum age limit for availing of the benefits of the scheme. There is no upper limit on annual income for availing of the pension. The applicant should not be receiving pension benefits under any other government scheme.

scheme_name: Chief Minister Ekal Nari Samman Pension Yojana
eligibility: The applicant's age should be 18 years or more. A native of the Rajasthan state. A widow/abandoned/divorced woman who does not have any source of regular income of her own for subsistence. Annual income from all sources should be less than Rs. 48000.

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act
eligibility: The applicant must be at least 18 years of age. The applicant must be residing in a Rural Area. The applicant must be an adult member of a rural household willing to do unskilled manual work.

All landholding farmer families in the country are eligible. The scheme excludes: institutional land holders; farmer families holding constitutional posts; serving or retired officers of State/Central Government; all serving or retired employees of Central/State PSEs and Government autonomous bodies; all superannuated/retired pensioners whose monthly pension is Rs.10000/- or more; all persons who paid income tax in last assessment year; professionals like doctors, engineers, lawyers, chartered accountants who are registered with professional bodies and are carrying out their profession.

Applicant should be an unorganised worker. Monthly income should be up to Rs. 15000. Age of entry is between 18 and 40 years. Applicant should not be covered under EPFO/ESIC/NPS (Government funded). The applicant should not be an income tax payer. A savings bank account/Jan Dhan account is mandatory.

The beneficiary family should not own a pucca house anywhere in India. EWS category: Annual household income up to Rs 3 lakh. LIG category: Annual household income between Rs 3 lakh and Rs 6 lakh. MIG-I: Annual income between Rs 6 lakh and Rs 12 lakh. MIG-II: Annual income between Rs 12 lakh and Rs 18 lakh. The applicant must reside in an urban area. Aadhaar is mandatory for benefit release.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana
eligibility: All AAY (Antyodaya Anna Yojana) and PHH (Priority Household) ration card holders under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) are eligible. Aadhaar seeding with the ration card is mandatory. The scheme provides free foodgrains in addition to the regular NFSA entitlement.

scheme_name: PM SVANidhi - PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi
eligibility: All street vendors who were vending in urban areas as on or before March 24 2020 are eligible. Vendors must be operating in urban areas. Certificate of Vending or Identity Card from ULB; or Letter of Recommendation (LoR) from ULB/Town Vending Committee is required. Working capital loan of up to Rs 10000 in first tranche, Rs 20000 in second tranche, Rs 50000 in third tranche.

Any Indian citizen who is 10 years old or above and does not have a bank account is eligible. Aadhaar is the primary KYC document for zero-balance account opening. Minor above 10 years may open account with guardian as co-holder. Account holders get a RuPay debit card with Rs. 1 lakh accident insurance cover and Rs. 30000 life insurance cover.

The LPG connection is issued in the name of the adult woman of the household. The applicant must be at least 18 years of age. Households belonging to BPL category as per SECC 2011 data or any poor household with self-declaration under PMUY 2.0. The family should not already have an LPG connection in its name. Deposit free LPG connection is provided.

Account can be opened in the name of a girl child from birth until she attains the age of 10 years. A parent or legal guardian can open a maximum of two accounts for two different girl children. Only one account per girl child. A post office or authorised bank is required for account opening. The account matures when the girl turns 21 or upon marriage after the age of 18.

All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing the notified crops in the notified areas are eligible. The applicant must have sown the insured crop in the notified area. Only notified crops in notified areas are insured under PMFBY. Claim settlement is done directly to the bank account of the farmer. Loanee farmers are compulsorily covered; non-loanee farmers may voluntarily opt in.

For Old Age Pension (NSAP-IGNOAPS): Age 60 years or above. Household must be below poverty line (BPL). For Widow Pension (NSAP-IGNWPS): Widow aged 40 years or above and BPL. For Disability Pension (NSAP-IGNDPS): Person with 80% or more disability and BPL. For Family Benefit (NSAP-NFBS): Death of primary breadwinner in BPL family between 18 and 64 years.

The scheme is open to students belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST). Students pursuing post-matriculation courses (Class 11 and above) in India are eligible. Annual income of the parent/guardian from all sources should not exceed Rs. 2.50 lakh. SC/ST certificate issued by competent authority is mandatory. Students must be Indian nationals.

Youth in the age group of 15-45 years are eligible. Students currently enrolled in full-time formal education are not eligible. Minimum educational qualification is Class 8 pass (varies by course). Bank account required for direct benefit transfer of training stipend. Training is provided through NSDC-affiliated training partners and sector skill councils.
